channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Eagles defensive star Josh Sweat believes he ‘should’ve’ been named Super Bowl LIX MVP
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles defensive star Josh Sweat said he "should've" been named Super Bowl LIX MVP. The award went to Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts after the Eagles' 40-22 win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL wide receiver Kadarius Toney is charged with assaulting a woman
item.description: DOUGLASVILLE, Ga. (AP) — NFL wide receiver Kadarius Toney has been charged with assaulting a woman in Georgia and preventing her from calling for help. Toney, 26, was arrested and booked into the Douglas County Jail on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trans athlete Sadie Schreiner not competing for RIT women's track team after Trump's executive order
item.description: Transgender track and field runner Sadie Schreiner is not competing in future women's track events for Rochester Institute of Technology amid a new NCAA policy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ricky Starks makes stunning WWE NXT appearance after release from rival company
item.description: Ricky Starks made his WWE debut on Tuesday as he appeared in the crowd at NXT a day after his release from All Elite Wrestling. He addressed fans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former coach Vilda denies pressure on Hermoso to downplay kiss after Women's World Cup final
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Jorge Vilda, the former coach of Spain women’s soccer team, told a judge on Wednesday that ex-soccer federation president Luis Rubiales did not ask him to put pressure on Jenni Hermoso following the outcry that erupted after Rubiales kissed the player.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cubs announce 2025 promotional calendar, featuring 5 new bobblehead giveaways
item.description: NOTE: The attached video clip is from a report by GN Sports' Josh Frydman this week from Cubs spring training camp in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago State begins search for head football coach
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The South Side's Chicago State University launched a search to find their first head football coach Tuesday. “We are excited to reach this milestone in our football planning.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Avery Neff makes stunning recovery from ankle injury
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Back January 17th, the #1 gymnastics recruit in the country, freshman Avery Neff was sailing along in her floor routine until her ankle buckled. The Huntsman Center went quiet as Neff had to be carried off the mat. Even she thought her season was over.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-MLB player Jacque Jones has high school jersey retired
item.description: Jacque Jones, a former Major League Baseball outfielder and San Diego native, had his legacy cemented in his alma mater's history on Saturday with the official retirement of his jersey number.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league. As president, he denigrated pros who took a knee during the national anthem as part of a social justice movement.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Utah HC gives up season-high 7 goals to Carolina
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (ABC4 Sports) - After two overtime thrilling victories, the Utah Hockey Club gave up a season-high seven goals in a 7-3 loss Saturday to the Carolina Hurricanes. Seth Jarvis had two goals and two assists, and Sebastian Aho had two goals and an assist for the Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Blackhawks visit the Blues after Donato's 2-goal performance
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks (17-31-6, in the Central Division) vs. St. Louis Blues (24-26-5, in the Central Division) St. Louis; Saturday, 7 p.m. EST BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Blues -250, Blackhawks +203; over/under is 5.5 BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks visit the St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl LIX: Chiefs, Eagles players with ties to San Diego area
item.description: Super Bowl Sunday is sure to be a fun one as the Philadelphia Eagles and Kansas City Chiefs battle it out for the Vince Lombardi Trophy.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods will play in Genesis Invitational at Torrey Pines
item.description: Tiger Woods, the host of The Genesis Invitational and an 82-time PGA Tour winner, will play at Torrey Pines Golf Course next week.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Baseball star Shohei Ohtani’s ex-interpreter is sentenced to nearly 5 years in sports betting case
item.description: SANTA ANA, Calif. (AP) — The former interpreter for baseball star Shohei Ohtani was sentenced Thursday to nearly five years in prison for bank and tax fraud after he stole nearly $17 million from the Los Angeles Dodgers player’s bank account.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NCAA changes transgender policy to limit women's competition to athletes assigned female at birth
item.description: The NCAA changed its participation policy for transgender athletes on Thursday, limiting competition in women's sports to athletes assigned female at birth only.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NCAA changes participation policy on transgender athletes
item.description: The NCAA has changed its participation policy for athletes, keeping transgender students from women's sports
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Butler headed to Golden State, Andrew Wiggins to Miami, AP source says
item.description: Jimmy Butler got what he wanted. He's being traded out of Miami and got a new contract in the process. The Heat and the Golden State Warriors have agreed on a deal that sends Butler to the Bay Area, a person with knowledge of the talks said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9
